free_text: Ni-58(P,P')Reaction at 60 MeV. Study of the Analyzing
Power For Inelastic Excitation of the Giant Resonance
Region of the Nuclear Continuum and of Low-Lying Bound
States.
Transformation to Lower Case. Some Reaction
Lines are Corrected.
Last checking has been done.
The Ni-58 Target was a 99.5 % Enriched, 27.8-Mg/Cm**2
Thick, Self-Supporting Foil About 5 Cm Wide by 2 Cm
Tall.
Collective-Model Distorted-Wave Born-Approximation
Calculations and Optical-Model Parameters.
.Reaction Protons were Detected by Nuclear
Emulsion Plates Placed in the Focal Plane of a Broad-
Range Magnetic Spectrograph. The Angular Acceptance
Of the Entrence Aperture to the Spectrograph was
+- 1 Angular Degree. Absorber Plates were Placed in
Front of the Emulsions to Remove All Reaction Products
Other the Protons.
.By CAJAD.
.The Uncertainty is Reported by Authors.
The Uncertainties in the Data Include Statistics and
An Estimated Uncertainty in the Number of Counts
Resulting from Possible Errors in Scanning the Nuclear
Emulsions. The Latter Uncertainty was 1-3% For Each
Spin  Direction  Depending Upon the Number of Tracks in
Each Scanning Bin.
.The Uncertainty is Reported by Authors.
.Data-Point Reader Uncertainty.

Were Treated As a Single State,Since They were not Well
Resolved at All Angles.
